title: CLAUDE.md
text: Framework: llegir R package utilizing ellmer LLM orchestration. Direct Imports: Core tools must depend strictly ModuleSet adapter, never directly hdWGCNA Seurat. Contracts: Every tool output must strictly validate evidence_fragment schema. Boilerplate Prose: Programmatic synthesis outputs must strictly match interpretation schema. free-form prose generation. Style Rules: Follow STYLE.md exactly (tidyverse, snake_case, 4-space indent, single quotes, roxygen comments unless explicitly asked).

title: Getting started with llegir
text: Every core tool depends ModuleSet adapter contract – [modules()], [gene_membership()], [module_scores()], [expression()], [metadata()], [pkg_versions()] – never specific backend package. [hdWGCNA_ModuleSet()] real-world adapter, built Seurat object run hdWGCNA. vignette instead use [llegir_example_moduleset()], small simulated dataset (two co-expressed gene modules plus background noise genes, 200 cells) satisfies contract, every tool runs unmodified . module_a simulated associate diagnosis column, module_b cell_type – used show real (null) evidence signal.
code: library(llegir) #>  #> Attaching package: 'llegir' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     capabilities, expression  ms <- llegir_example_moduleset() modules(ms) #> [1] "module_a" "module_b" head(gene_membership(ms, 'module_a')) #>    gene_name   module       kme #> 4     GENEA4 module_a 0.9389462 #> 9     GENEA9 module_a 0.9368404 #> 5     GENEA5 module_a 0.9330942 #> 10   GENEA10 module_a 0.9324491 #> 8     GENEA8 module_a 0.9304695 #> 1     GENEA1 module_a 0.9299657 head(metadata(ms)) #>       diagnosis cell_type  sample #> cell1      case myeloid_b sample1 #> cell2   control myeloid_b sample1 #> cell3      case myeloid_a sample3 #> cell4      case myeloid_b sample2 #> cell5   control myeloid_b sample3 #> cell6      case myeloid_b sample4

title: Getting started with llegir
text: tool takes ctx <- list(ms, module_id, params) returns single, validated evidence_fragment – see [evidence_fragment()] shipped evidence_fragment.schema.json full contract. fourth core tool, [geneset_enrichment_tool()], runs offline GO/pathway enrichment local GMT file (ctx$params$db_files); ’s omitted since gene-set library ships package, follows ctx -> evidence_fragment contract tools . User-supplied result tables (e.g. DME table computed outside llegir) can folded pipeline via [import_fragment()] / [import_fragment_tool()], tagged provenance$source = 'user_supplied' rather 'computed'.
code: ctx <- list(ms = ms, module_id = 'module_a', params = list(n_hubs = 10)) hub_frag <- hub_genes_tool(ctx) validate_evidence_fragment(hub_frag) hub_frag$compact_summary #> [1] "top 10 hub genes by kME: GENEA4, GENEA9, GENEA5, GENEA10, GENEA8, GENEA1, GENEA2, GENEA7, GENEA3, GENEA6" meta_frag <- module_by_metadata_tool(list(     ms = ms, module_id = 'module_a',     params = list(column = 'diagnosis', column_type = 'categorical') )) meta_frag$compact_summary #> [1] "diagnosis (cell-level): strongest group case (r=0.8, FDR=9.5e-23); omnibus Kruskal p=9.4e-23" cluster_frag <- cluster_dme_tool(list(     ms = ms, module_id = 'module_b',     params = list(group_by = 'cell_type') )) cluster_frag$compact_summary #> [1] "strongest state: myeloid_a (r=0.86, FDR=1.1e-25); omnibus Kruskal p=1.1e-25"

title: Getting started with llegir
text: [run_module()] runs configured list tools one module bundles fragments validated, hashed evidence packet – [build_evidence_packet()] hood. packet_hash content hash fragments (timestamps excluded), ’s reproducibility fingerprint: evidence always hashes way. [run_orchestrator()] every module ModuleSet , writing one packet JSON per module (plus, optionally, every fragment’s full result table via tables_dir): single module failing (e.g. degenerate module tripping one tool) doesn’t take whole batch – ’s warned recorded NULL, leaving every module’s packet intact.
code: tool_config <- list(     list(fn = hub_genes_tool, params = list(n_hubs = 10)),     list(fn = module_by_metadata_tool, params = list(column = 'diagnosis', column_type = 'categorical')) ) packet <- run_module(ms, 'module_a', tool_config, input_hash = 'vignette-example') packet$packet_hash #> [1] "f4ced8450d61cbef823fbdd533dc32bb37b5803252ebc593d1510c23b3c25732" length(packet$fragments) #> [1] 2 out_dir <- tempfile('packets_') packets <- run_orchestrator(ms, tool_config, output_dir = out_dir) list.files(out_dir) #> [1] "module_a.json" "module_b.json"

title: Getting started with llegir
text: Synthesis requires [dataset_description()] – biological context prepended every prompt, model interprets module right frame (e.g. CSF myeloid vs. tumor tissue changes given program means). ’s hard requirement, default: [validate_dataset_description()] errors missing/empty required field.
code: desc <- dataset_description(     species = 'human', tissue = 'simulated tissue', cell_compartment = 'simulated cells',     assay = 'simulated scRNA-seq', conditions = c('case', 'control') ) cat(render_dataset_description(desc)) #> Dataset context: #> - species: human #> - tissue: simulated tissue #> - cell compartment: simulated cells #> - assay: simulated scRNA-seq #> - conditions: case, control

title: Getting started with llegir
text: [synthesize_module()] runs full packet -> interpretation pipeline: calls backend via [synthesize_interpretation()], enforces citation faithfulness ([enforce_faithfulness()] – every claim’s fragment_ids must actually exist packet, direction must match), fuses model deterministic confidence ([fuse_confidence()]). model’s role bounded design: ever sees compact prompt built [build_system_prompt()] / [build_user_prompt()] (fragment summaries top findings, never raw result tables), never runs analysis code. , [mock_backend()] stands live model – canned, fixed response never touches network, used whole test suite vignette:
code: interp <- synthesize_module(packet, desc, backend = mock_backend()) interp$proposed_label #> [1] "Myeloid activation program (mock)" interp$confidence$score #> [1] 0.5731577 cat(render_paragraph(interp)) #> **Myeloid activation program (mock)** (module_a) #>  #> Mock synthesis output for offline testing; not derived from the evidence packet. #>  #> Dominant biology: Not evaluated by the mock backend. #>  #> Supporting evidence: #> - Hub genes were computed by the deterministic core. (hub_genes; direction: na) #> - The module has enriched gene-set terms. (geneset_enrichment; direction: up) #>  #> Confidence: 0.57 -- Mock backend: fixed neutral confidence, not evidence-derived. [fusion: model=0.50, evidence=0.65 (effect=0.94, enrichment=0.00, agreement=1.00/convergent_signal), fused=0.57] #> Flags: needs_human_review

title: Getting started with llegir
text: llegir’s synthesis backends pluggable via [resolve_backend()] (provider = 'github' 'gemini', built [ellmer_backend()]) [ellmer_backend()] directly ellmer::chat_*() constructor. need network access provider API key (picked ellmer provider’s environment variable, e.g. GITHUB_PAT GEMINI_API_KEY), aren’t exercised vignette R CMD check – shown reference: [cached_backend()] wraps backend -disk cache keyed packet hash + provider + model + prompt template version, re-running synthesis unchanged packet never re-spends API call.
code: backend <- resolve_backend(provider = 'gemini') interp <- synthesize_module(packet, desc, backend = backend)

what: 6. Batch synthesis: review queue and manifest
title: Getting started with llegir
text: [run_synthesis_orchestrator()] runs [synthesize_module()] batch packets (e.g. output [run_orchestrator()]), writing per-module interpretation JSON rendered Markdown paragraphs, plus run-level review_queue.tsv manifest.json: review queue ([build_review_queue()]) lists flagged interpretations ([needs_review()]) – triage queue human reviewer, summary every module – sorted lowest-confidence first: mock backend always returns fixed, generic content regardless actual evidence packet – citations confidence score never truly grounded vignette’s per-module evidence, needs_human_review expected . live model, synthesizing real packet contents, typically agree deterministic evidence often flag fewer modules.
code: synth_dir <- tempfile('synthesis_') interps <- run_synthesis_orchestrator(packets, desc, backend = mock_backend(), output_dir = synth_dir) list.files(synth_dir) #> [1] "manifest.json"    "module_a.json"    "module_a.md"      "module_b.json"    #> [5] "module_b.md"      "review_queue.tsv" build_review_queue(interps) #>          module_id confidence              flags #> module_b  module_b      0.491 needs_human_review #> module_a  module_a      0.573 needs_human_review #>                                                                                       reason #> module_b model confidence and deterministic evidence disagree, or a citation issue was found #> module_a model confidence and deterministic evidence disagree, or a citation issue was found

what: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
title: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
text: fixed set rules governing backend must fill model-facing interpretation schema: evidence-claims, citation requirements, direction consistency, controlled vocabularies, empty-literature constraint.

title: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
text: single character string.

title: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
code: cat(build_system_prompt()) #> You are filling a structured interpretation of one gene co-expression module #> from a fixed evidence packet produced by a deterministic analysis pipeline. #>  #> Rules: #> - Use only the evidence given below. Do not invent genes, terms, or results, and do not run or imagine any analysis. #> - Every entry in supporting_claims must cite the fragment_id(s) it is based on, and its direction must match the direction reported by those fragments. #> - A single supporting_claims entry may only cite fragment_ids that all share the same direction. ranked_genes fragments (e.g. hub_genes) always report direction na, so never combine one in the same claim as a directional fragment (e.g. geneset_enrichment, direction up/down) -- cite them as separate supporting_claims entries instead, each using the direction its own fragment(s) actually report. #> - metadata_associations entries must cite a real fragment_id the same way. #> - Each fragment has a type from a controlled vocabulary: ranked_genes, categorical_association, continuous_correlation, geneset_enrichment, signature_correlation, cross_condition_delta, state_expression. #> - flags must be drawn only from: insufficient_evidence, needs_human_review, possible_artifact, tool_conflict, label_low_specificity. #> - If the evidence is weak, sparse, or inconsistent, do not invent a confident story: set flags to include insufficient_evidence, keep supporting_claims minimal (or empty), and give a low confidence score. #> - confidence.score is your own calibrated estimate in [0, 1] of how well-supported proposed_label is by the evidence below. #> - literature must be left empty; literature grounding is not available in this pipeline.

what: Compute deterministic evidence signals from a packet — compute_evidence_signals
title: Compute deterministic evidence signals from a packet — compute_evidence_signals
text: Signals independent model output: maximum bounded effect strength across fragments, number significant enrichment terms, cross-tool agreement whether module shows real signal. Two guards known false-positive patterns (thresholds tuned one dataset): fragment counts "signal" clears significance effect-size bar (large-N tests can hit p~0 practically negligible effect), enrichment term counts significant least min_overlap_genes overlapping genes (single-gene overlap narrow term classic false positive small hub-gene list).

Experimental: public API yet stable.

what: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
text: co-variation sibling geneset_enrichment_tool(): overlap asks whether module contains signature's genes; tool asks whether module's activity co-varies signature's. Scores every signature ctx$params$library_files across cells via UCell::ScoreSignatures_UCell() decoupleR::run_ulm() (scoring gene_list_ModuleSet() uses), correlates signature's score module's module_scores().

title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
text: ctx tool context list: list(ms, module_id, params), built run_module(). ctx$params$library_files (required) named character vector local .gmt .rds signature-library file paths, e.g. c(Hallmark = 'data/h..v2026.1.Hs.symbols.gmt'). .rds file must contain named list character vectors (gene sets); .gmt read via fgsea::gmtPathways(). ctx$params$method 'UCell' (default) 'decoupleR'. ctx$params$sample_col (default 'sample') sample-id column used sample-level correlation.

title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
text: evidence_fragment type 'signature_correlation', NULL ctx$ms lacks module_scores expression capability (see capabilities()) – graceful skip, error.

title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
